rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can reset epoch 0 to zero and corrupt the first live epoch’s denominator
rejected_candidates.reason: Unsupported as a reportable issue: any epoch-0 `deposit()` already initializes `poolSize[token][1]` from the live balance, so later resetting epoch 0 does not retroactively zero the first rewarded epoch. Epoch 0 itself is explicitly a bootstrap epoch with no rewards.

rejected_candidates.title: Bootstrap deposits more than one epoch early receive multipliers above 100%
rejected_candidates.reason: The >100% multiplier only affects epoch-0 effective-balance math. `deposit()` sets epoch 1 pool size from the raw token balance, not from the multiplied checkpoint value, so the claimed propagation into the first live epoch is not supported by the code.
